what_it_does: Defines PlatformApp, the root React component for a platform UI kit. It holds an `active` screen-id in state (default 'gallery'), switches on it to render one of four real screens (Gallery, CalendarScreen, BrandKit, HubSettings) or else a generic Stub fed a title and note, inside a shell layout with a sidebar, top bar, and chats widget. It then attaches PlatformApp to window and mounts it into the #app element if present.
observations: STUB_NOTES maps six unbuilt screen ids (projects, dashboard, landing, files, templates, pricing) to descriptive placeholder notes shown via the Stub component
Only four screens are real (gallery, calendar, brandkit, hubsettings); everything else falls through to Stub
Uses React via global `React`/`ReactDOM` and destructures useState as `useState_app` (aliased to avoid bundle collisions)
titleFor() returns a human title for stub ids, falling back to the id itself
Renders TopBar with notifications={7} and Chats with count={21} as hardcoded literals
standouts: The stub-vs-real split: descriptive STUB_NOTES strings document intended screens that don't exist yet, doubling as inline design notes
conventions: Follows the project's no-self-mounting-poison guard (DOM-guards createRoot per CLAUDE.md trap)
Aliases useState to a unique name, consistent with sharing one bundle namespace
Attaches component to window for the global-script consumption pattern
concerns: Hardcoded notification (7) and chat (21) counts inline rather than via state or props from a source
Screen routing is an if/else chain rather than a registry/map, so adding a screen means editing this file
notes: The platform UI kit's root router: four working screens plus stubbed placeholders for the rest, mounted into #app.
questions: Where do TopBar's notifications count and Chats' count come from in a real build, given they are hardcoded here?
Are the six STUB_NOTES screens planned for this kit or just documentation?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — comment says 'root app with screen routing' and that is exactly what it does.
intention_signals: Aims to be a runnable platform shell demonstrating navigation across product screens, with placeholders standing in for screens not yet implemented so the whole nav is walkable.
novelty: The STUB_NOTES pattern that turns unbuilt screens into documented placeholders inside the router itself.
contribution: Contributes a platform-shell root composition (sidebar + main + topbar + chats) and an explicit screen-id catalogue covering projects, dashboard, landing, files, templates, pricing, plus the four built screens.
summary_for_embedding: Root React component PlatformApp that routes between four built screens and stubbed placeholders inside a sidebar/topbar/chats shell, mounted to #app.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a runnable platform UI shell with walkable navigation across all product screens, real ones rendered and unbuilt ones documented as stubs.
